title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s are frustrating, especially when permissions haven't changed.

When you get the 403, does it happen:

1. **On initial page load** — you navigate to the dashboard and immediately get a 403
2. **Mid-session** — the dashboard loads fine, but then a 403 appears when you click something or after some time
3. **On refresh** — you get a 403, refresh the page, and it works (or vice versa)
4. **Something else**

This will help narrow down whether it's a routing/load-balancing issue vs. something in the auth layer.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It's mostly option 3. I'll navigate to the dashboard and get a 403 right away, then I refresh and it works fine. Sometimes I have to refresh a couple times. It does seem like maybe 1 out of 3 times I get the error? I assumed it was some kind of network issue on my end but my coworker who also just got the new analyst role says the same thing is happening to her.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — the refresh behavior strongly suggests a load balancer or caching issue rather than a network problem on your end.

You mentioned your coworker "just got the new analyst role." Did you also recently get that role (or any role change), and was the timing around when the 403s started a couple days ago?

1. **Yes** — I (or both of us) got the analyst role right around when this started
2. **No** — I've had my current role for a while; the 403s started independently
3. **Not sure** — I'd have to check with my admin

I'm asking because the 1-in-3 failure pattern could point to some servers not recognizing the new role yet.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah, now that you mention it, I think it was option 1. I got the analyst role maybe two or three days ago — right around when this started happening. My coworker got hers around the same time too. I didn't really connect the two things until you asked, but the timing does line up. So you think some of the servers don't know about the analyst role yet?

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors for users with newly assigned 'analyst' role — likely role cache/propagation inconsistency across server instances","problem":"Users who were recently assigned the 'analyst' role are experiencing intermittent 403 Forbidden errors when loading the TaskFlow dashboard. Approximately 1 in 3 page loads fail, but refreshing typically resolves the error. Multiple users with the same new role are affected. The issue began 2-3 days ago, coinciding with when the role was assigned.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The 'analyst' role permissions are not consistently propagated or cached across all application server instances behind the load balancer. When a request hits an instance that has the updated role/permissions, the dashboard loads normally. When it hits an instance with stale role data, the user gets a 403 because that instance doesn't recognize the analyst role as authorized for the dashboard.","reproduction_steps":["Assign a user the 'analyst' role","Have that user navigate to the TaskFlow dashboard","Repeat page loads — approximately 1 in 3 should return a 403 Forbidden","Refresh the page — the dashboard should load on a subsequent attempt"],"environment":"Production environment, behind a load balancer with multiple server instances. Issue affects multiple users who recently received the 'analyst' role.","severity":"high","impact":"All users newly assigned the 'analyst' role are unable to reliably access the dashboard. The workaround (refreshing) is available but disruptive. This likely affects any new analyst role assignments going forward.","recommended_fix":"Investigate the role/permission caching and propagation mechanism across server instances. Check: (1) whether the analyst role was recently added to the system and if all instances have the updated role definitions, (2) whether there is a permissions cache with a stale TTL or missing invalidation on role assignment, (3) whether a rolling restart or cache clear resolves the issue across all instances. Also verify the analyst role is correctly mapped to dashboard access in the authorization layer.","proposed_test_case":"Assign the analyst role to a test user, then make 20+ sequential requests to the dashboard endpoint and verify all return 200. Additionally, add an integration test that assigns a new role and immediately verifies access across multiple simulated backend instances.","information_gaps":["Whether users without the analyst role (or with longer-standing roles) are also affected","Number of server instances behind the load balancer","Whether the analyst role is a newly created role vs. a pre-existing one"]}
